notes: The ShopiFixer page posts to /__public-checkout or an env-based base URL plus /__public-checkout.
The route creates a Stripe Checkout session and binds packet payment_pending state.
The webhook already verifies Stripe signatures with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
The webhook already extracts packet_id and store_domain from checkout.session.completed metadata.
The webhook already calls recordStripePaymentPropagation after bindPacketPayment.

smallest_repair: Add a runtime-safe fulfillment update helper, then invoke it from web/src/routes/stripeWebhook.esm.js after payment propagation succeeds so the paid client record produces payment_received in shopifixer_fulfillment_truth_v1.json.

payment_completion_bridge.current_gap: Payment truth is written, but fulfillment truth is still rebuilt offline rather than updated by the runtime payment path.
payment_completion_bridge.smallest_safe_repair: After recordStripePaymentPropagation succeeds in the Stripe webhook, call the fulfillment truth builder or extract its core fulfillment update logic into a runtime-safe helper so the paid client record produces payment_received in shopifixer_fulfillment_truth_v1.json.
preferred_implementation_path: Keep the existing Stripe webhook and payment writers.
Add a small runtime helper that maps paid client truth to fulfillment truth.
Invoke that helper from web/src/routes/stripeWebhook.esm.js after successful payment propagation.

implementation_allowed_next: Add a runtime-safe fulfillment update helper.
Invoke the helper from web/src/routes/stripeWebhook.esm.js after payment propagation succeeds.
Keep checkout, Stripe, client registry, revenue truth, and fulfillment truth semantics unchanged.
blockers: Fulfillment truth is still updated by an offline builder rather than a runtime bridge.
The smallest safe repair needs a runtime-safe helper or bridge before payment can advance to fulfillment truth.
